titleEn: Fieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robot
titleZh: Fieldwork Robotics获新融资，加速树莓采摘机器人商业化
summaryZh: 英国Fieldwork Robotics获得SEED Innovations等投资方共250万英镑种子+轮融资，用于加速其自主树莓采摘机器人的商业化部署。该机器人已在英国商业农场测试，计划2027年实现多机集群商业运营，并拓展至澳大利亚。技术旨在解决劳动力短缺导致的果实损失问题，降低采摘成本并提升产量。
translationZh:
总部位于英国的Fieldwork Robotics已从SEED Innovations获得额外投资，以加速其自主树莓采摘机器人的商业部署。SEED Innovations是一家投资公司，由企业家Jim Mellon担任董事长。

该投资是250万英镑（约290万欧元/340万美元）种子+轮融资的一部分。此前该公司已于2026年4月完成一轮220万英镑（约250万欧元/300万美元）融资，此外还获得了Innovate UK和一项气候补助金提供的170万英镑（约200万欧元/230万美元）资助。

**从技术验证转向商业农场部署**

Fieldwork Robotics是普利茅斯大学的衍生公司，专注于开发用于采摘新鲜树莓的选择性模块化机器人。该公司目前正通过为期两年的Innovate UK ADOPT项目，在诺福克郡的Place UK和斯塔福德郡的Littywood Farm部署生产型机器人。据首席执行官David Fulton称，这笔额外资金将支持公司从技术验证转向商业农场部署。该公司计划在澳大利亚开展试验，拓展英国以外的市场，并预计从2027年起实现多台机器人集群的商业化运营。

**降低采摘成本**

SEED Innovations董事长Jim Mellon表示，该技术解决了浆果种植者面临的最大挑战之一。他指出，劳动力短缺可能导致大量果实损失，而自主采摘提供了一种可扩展的解决方案来提高生产力。Fieldwork表示，其目标是实现与人工采摘相当的果实品质，同时降低采摘成本，使种植者能够扩大生产规模。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了机器人采摘技术在果蔬领域的突破性进展，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解自动化采摘在种植业中的落地路径、融资模式及规模化前景具有重要参考价值。
contentFull: Fieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robot 25-06 | Smart farming | NewsFieldwork Robotics is currently testing its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot on commercial farms in the UK. The company expects fleets of multiple machines to enter commercial operation from 2027. Photo: Fieldwork Robotics.UK-based Fieldwork Robotics has secured additional investment from SEED Innovations, the investment company chaired by entrepreneur Jim Mellon, to accelerate the commercial deployment of its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot.The investment forms part of a £2.5 million (€2.9 million / $3.4 million) Seed+ funding round. It follows a previous £2.2 million (€2.5 million / $3.0 million) investment round completed in April 2026 and is complemented by £1.7 million (€2.0 million / $2.3 million) in grants from Innovate UK and a Climate Grant.Transition from technology validation to commercial farm deploymentFieldwork Robotics, a spin-out from the University of Plymouth, develops selective and modular robots for harvesting fresh raspberries. The company is currently deploying production machines through a 2-year Innovate UK ADOPT programme with Place UK in Norfolk and Littywood Farm in Staffordshire. According to CEO David Fulton, the additional funding will support the transition from technology validation to commercial farm deployment. The company plans to expand beyond the UK with trials in Australia and expects fleets of multiple robots to be operating commercially from 2027.Reducing harvesting costsSEED Innovations’ chairman Jim Mellon said the technology addresses one of the biggest challenges facing berry growers. He noted that labour shortages can lead to significant fruit losses and that autonomous harvesting offers a scalable solution to improve productivity. Fieldwork says it aims to deliver fruit quality comparable to that achieved by human pickers while reducing harvesting costs and enabling growers to scale production. author: Geert Hekkert
contentHtml: UK-based Fieldwork Robotics has secured additional investment from SEED Innovations, the investment company chaired by entrepreneur Jim Mellon, to accelerate the commercial deployment of its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot.
